content: The existing Self-Refine framework, which requires no additional training or supervised data to iteratively improve LLM outputs via self-feedback, can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break the framework's tendency to get stuck in local optimization traps caused by single-perspective feedback. This creates a hybrid self-improvement pipeline that retains Self-Refine's practical advantage of no extra training while boosting refinement quality by exposing blind spots inherent to a single model's initial reasoning frame.
